target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Channel echo / session pinning (pin-from-here) with native Telegram rendering A session can mirror its turns to other threads that are bound to the **same** session, so a long run you started in one place can be followed from another — and the mirror renders **natively** in the destination channel (live draft stream, not a flat `[echo]` copy). ### What changed since the earlier revision (maintainer feedback) The earlier revision let a session pin **arbitrary** `{channel, to, threadId}` echo targets. The maintainer's concern was that continuous mirroring to arbitrary chat ids is a heavy primitive and lacks an authorization story for streaming conversation content to any chat id. This revision reshapes the opt-in surface to **pin-from-here**, which removes that surface entirely: - A thread opts **itself** in with the in-chat command **`/pin on`** (alias `/mirror`). It can only mirror the session it is **already bound to** — there is no arbitrary-target argument — so a pin can never cross a session boundary. Every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that exact session. **Zero new authorization surface.** - The operator add paths (`sessions echo` CLI + `sessions.echo` RPC) now **reject any target that is not a verified participant** of the session (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), with guidance to use `/pin` from the target thread instead. The "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path is gone. - Self-exclusion is correct by construction: `/pin` captures the thread's own origin identity (the exact fields the fan-out uses as a turn's origin), so when this thread drives a turn it is excluded from its own fan-out; when a sibling thread of the same session drives, this thread receives the mirror. `last*` (the session's persistent external delivery route) is unchanged — a transient webchat/cron poke still must not hijack an external session's route (session-delivery.ts, #47745). ### Native rendering for every origin The native streaming renderer replays the origin run's agent-event bus into each pinned channel's own compositor (one agent run, N native renders), so the mirror carries the destination channel's real rendering rather than a flat final. This revision also fixes a latent bug so the native path engages for **non- channel-origin** turns (webchat `chat.send`, cron/heartbeat): the fan-out derived its origin from the session's `last*`, which those turns deliberately do not claim — so the origin defaulted to the previously-active channel and, when that channel was itself the pinned target, the fan-out **self-excluded** it and fell back to the flat post-hoc mirror. It now derives the fan-out origin from the turn's **actual** origin (`OriginatingChannel/To`), falling back to `last*`, so a webchat trigger renders natively in the pinned channel. ### New in this revision (3 commits on top of the existing channel-echo work) - `feat(reply): add /pin (pin-from-here) in-chat command` — `commands-pin.ts` + registration; stores the caller's origin identity into the bound session's echo targets. Unit tests incl. an end-to-end check chaining `/pin` through the real `resolveEchoTargets` fan-out (sibling included, self excluded). - `feat(echo): reject arbitrary-target echo adds; require a session participant` — `targetMatchesSessionParticipant` in `echo.ts`, wired into both operator add paths. Unit tests for accept/reject/fail-closed. - `fix(echo): native streaming fan-out uses the turn's actual origin, not stale last*` — `agent-runner-execution.ts`. The existing channel-echo infrastructure (native streaming fan-out, mirror resolver, Telegram renderer, owner-bound `registerEchoRendererFactory`, the types-only `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath) is unchanged by these three. ### Tests + CI (current head `d6c154cb22f`) - `pnpm tsgo:core` + `pnpm tsgo:test:src`: 0 errors. `oxlint` on the changed files: 0 warnings / 0 errors. - Unit tests for the changed surface pass, including the **152-test `agent-runner-execution.test`** suite the origin fix touches, plus `commands-pin.test`, `echo.test`, and `chat.send-user-echo.test`. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow with a local patched crabbox: a real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch (`OpenClaw 2026.5.31 d6c154c`, global-installed) + Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, run locally — driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with `/verbose on` and an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread and webchat are bound to one session; the thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt, and the pinned thread receives that webchat-origin turn — including the real tool result — rendered natively. Captured sequence (real Telegram Desktop, see image/video below): 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Run the shell command: date -u — use your exec tool, then tell me the UTC time…` — the webchat-origin, tool-using prompt 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The current UTC time is 14:35:12 on Thursday, June 11, 2026."** — i.e. a real `date -u` exec, run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread. **Video (inline player):** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cadf32f9-bdea-494f-b8dd-98c0a4c37d9f Screenshot (final frame — the webchat-triggered `date -u` reply in the pinned thread): If the inline player does not load, the same clip is also here: [MP4](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-telegram.mp4) · [GIF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof/pin-from-here-evidence/.proof/pin-from-here-telegram-motion.gif) Native-path engagement on the same build was confirmed by instrumenting the fan-out: for the webchat-origin turn `resolveEchoTargets` returns the Telegram target (origin = webchat, not self-excluded), the factory is found, and the renderer is **created** (native). Gateway log for the filmed run: `session turn created … channel=webchat` → the real claude turn ran the exec tool (`outcome=completed`) and the result rendered in the pinned thread. Session store after `/pin`: `echoTargets: [{"channel":"telegram","to":"telegram:-1003900553563","threadId":1,"label":"pinned"}]`. **Proof environment:** single-node local gateway, one Telegram backend, isolated state dir; real `claude-cli` model with exec tools enabled. Sensitive chat ids are the test supergroup, not production. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). Implementation, tests, and the local behavior-proof capture were AI-assisted; framing and the proof are the author's.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
commands.command: re-review — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. the operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetmatchessessionparticipant`). also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`originatingchannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror
commands.freeform_prompt: — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. The operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`). Also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`OriginatingChannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror.
